video_frame_merged_caption:
In a professional newsroom setting, a Black female news anchor with long black hair stands to the right of a large display framed by an illuminated gray surround with a blue border that matches her dark blue sleeveless dress with a square neckline. Wearing a wedding ring, she looks directly into the camera, gestures as she speaks, and at times clasps her hands. Behind her, a desk and computer are visible, and a TV screen in the upper right shows a man in a suit. The large screen beside her presents the headline “VETOES & CHALLENGES” in white beside an image of a white voting booth bearing an American flag and the word “VOTE,” and also incorporates a solar panel image. In another view of the same setup, the display reads “Vetoes and Challenges” in white, with the same visual pairing of a voting booth labeled “VOTE” with the American flag and a solar panel, underscoring the segment’s focus. The color palette across the set and graphics features shades of gray, blue, white, black, red, and yellow.

The report moves into a split-screen presentation. On the left, a blue background carries the site label “10TampaBay.com” at the top and a white horizontal strip with blue text that reads “VETOED.” Beneath the strip, two bullet points in white text state: “Bill would have phased out current solar net metering,” and “Metering is the rate people get paid for using solar, sending leftovers to the grid.” On the right, solar panels fill the view: eight panels appear, each divided into five grids. Additional rooftop footage shows a gray building covered with numerous black solar panels outlined by white lines, with some sections forming a stair-step arrangement. In the upper right of that shot, a green circle with the number “10” appears with the word “drone” above it.

The visuals tighten to the system hardware. A meter locked in a gray metal box appears, with two red labels affixed to the box. Part of a solar photovoltaic array, the device registers the passage rate of solar energy, and its readings reflect the amount of energy being processed. A closer view presents a solar meter with a white background and a digital display reading “99172.” Above the display is a red label with a white barcode. The meter is encased in a white plastic frame, and a small silver metal piece is visible at the bottom right corner.

The split-screen returns to solar installations, including a white brick building adorned with numerous grid-arranged panels, each panel featuring a white border and a black surface. A “Drone 10” logo sits in the top right, designed to resemble a drone, with the number “10” in white and the drone in light green.

The segment concludes with a full graphic that pairs an official portrait of Governor Ron DeSantis with a text panel. On the left, he appears smiling, a white man with short brown hair, wearing a black suit jacket, white collared shirt, and yellow tie, with a red and gold pin on his lapel; the American flag stands behind him against a blue wall, and “10TampaBay.com” displays in white in the upper left. On the right, a blue background carries his statement in white: “The state of Florida should not contribute to the financial crunch that our citizens are experiencing.” The attribution appears in a white rectangle with blue text: “Governor Ron DeSantis (R-FL).”
video_transcript:
The video shows a news reporter standing beside a large screen display in a news studio. She is dressed in a dark blue sleeveless dress with a square neckline and the blue of her dress matches the blue decorative frame around the screen. On the screen we see the words VETOES & CHALLENGES written in white color of text.
 The news report continues, as we see on the large screen, a split display. The frame to the left has a blue background. It has a bulleted list on it, talking about a bill that was vetoed, we see the main bullet points of the bill, below. To the right side of the split screen we see some solar panels.
 Next we see the meter box of a solar photovoltaic array AC and a Solar meter running.
 we see more of the solar panels, and lastly we see a profile image of the governor of Florida, Governor Ron DeSantis, with a quote that says, The state of Florida should not contribute to the financial crunch that our citizens are experiencing.
